# Ant Code v1.1 Experience Acceptance

Date: 2026-04-28

Package:

- Version: `1.1.0`
- Tarball: `dist/ant-code-cli-1.1.0.tgz`
- SHA256: record externally after packaging so the package does not contain a
  self-referential hash.

## Scope

This acceptance note covers the first experience parity build after the v1.0
internal release. The main goal is to reduce the gap between the safe clean-room
client and the smoother interaction style expected from a daily coding agent.

## Accepted Changes

- Live OpenAI-compatible SSE deltas are forwarded through the gateway and
  session event pipeline.
- The TUI renders assistant output while it streams.
- Provider-exposed thinking/reasoning deltas are shown when the configured
  local adapter sends them.
- Tool-call argument streaming and local tool run status are visible in the TUI.
- The TUI startup surface now presents Ant Code identity, model, gateway,
  permission mode, network mode, and the local tool boundary.
- The prompt composer now includes slash command discovery, workspace file
  mentions, local shell mode, queued prompts while busy, permission-mode
  cycling, and richer approval modal text.
- The TUI responds to terminal resize events and shows the Ant Code logo on the
  initial trust/startup surface instead of collapsing it into a one-line log.
- `/model` now opens a TUI selector, while `/model list` and `/model use
  <model-id>` expose the same local gateway aliases for direct command use.
- Product commands for model, cost/usage, context, keybindings, local sessions,
  memory, MCP, and agents have clear local behavior; deferred advanced
  workflow commands return explicit lab-local disabled states.
- The implementation remains independently authored and uses reviewed
  open-source TUI dependencies only.

## Validation

Commands run:

```sh
npm run verify:release
node src/cli/index.js --version
node src/cli/index.js -p "Reply exactly: sonnet-ready"
```

Additional local interactive stream smoke:

```json
{
  "output": "stream-ready",
  "streamEventCount": 2,
  "eventTypes": [
    "turn_start",
    "gateway_request_start",
    "gateway_stream_start",
    "assistant_delta",
    "gateway_stream_stop",
    "gateway_response",
    "assistant_final",
    "turn_complete"
  ]
}
```

`npm run verify:release` passed with 176 tests.

## 2026-04-29 Continuation Note

The follow-up TUI polish pass tightened shortcut and `/guide` product copy:

- `/guide <message>` is documented as interrupting the active turn and running
  guidance next, not as same-request live injection.
- FooterBar and `/keybindings` now include the real `Esc`/`Ctrl+G` interrupt
  path and keep `Ctrl+C` scoped to two-press TUI exit confirmation.
- Small-window scrollback layout has deterministic unit coverage for expanded
  transcript/live row budgets without fixed pane height.

Focused validation passed:

```sh
npm test -- tests/unit/tui-frame.test.js tests/unit/tui-format.test.js tests/unit/commands.test.js tests/unit/tui-workflows.test.js
```

Full continuation validation also passed with 218 tests:

```sh
npm run check
```

After live user feedback on April 29, 2026, another follow-up patch addressed
large-window duplicate stale frames and small-window mouse wheel snap-back by
clearing terminal scrollback on startup/resize, removing idle pulse redraws, and
leaving raw wheel events to native terminal scrollback in small-window mode.
Small-window scrolling was later reported normal; large-window mouse wheel
reachability was then improved by enabling native scrollback rendering for all
terminal heights and rendering the full transcript row count instead of a fixed
large-window pane.

Real-terminal manual validation later confirmed the Stage 4-6 TUI surfaces:
long streaming transcript review, mouse-wheel scrolling, right-side panel
stability, `Ctrl+O` detail expansion, tool/write permission confirmation, and
common command product panels.

The next April 29 continuation pass addressed two live-feedback issues:

- `/resume` now accepts a unique visible UUID prefix and restores bounded
  retained user/assistant transcript messages when local transcript retention is
  enabled. Legacy metadata-only records still resume id/turn state with zero
  restored messages.
- The `/sessions` picker shows readable titles derived from the first prompt or
  retained transcript, with short ids retained as secondary disambiguators.
- `Ctrl+O` compact/detail/full now visibly changes transcript rendering for long
  assistant paragraphs even when the model emitted them as a single line.

Focused validation passed:

```sh
node --test tests/unit/storage.test.js tests/unit/session.test.js tests/unit/tui-format.test.js tests/unit/tui-command-panels.test.js
```

Full validation also passed with 220 tests:

```sh
npm run check
```

## Residual Gap

This build now has accepted core TUI parity through the Stage 4-6 experience
surfaces. Remaining work is Stage 7-9 depth: final file/session/queue workflow
smoke, richer background/task and extension panels, release hardening, and
broader terminal regression captures across real terminals.
